claims.claim: In a controlled synthetic experiment using exact Markov target and draft distributions with intentional domain-shift mismatches, we compare RSD against baseline (no distillation), first-reject-only distillation, uniform on-policy knowledge distillation, and uniform teacher-sampled distillation.

evidence_refs.quote: Speculative decoding accelerates autoregressive inference by proposing multiple draft tokens and verifying them against a target model, discarding any suffix beyond the first rejection. We investigate whether the contexts associated with these rejected spans provide a useful distillation curriculum—*rejection-span distillation* (RSD)—for aligning a draft model to its target.
